# pdf2htmlEX Full AI Repository Guide

This file is optimized for AI agents doing code modifications in this local
repository. It summarizes the current codebase only.

## Repository Purpose

`pdf2htmlEX` converts PDF files into HTML. It attempts to preserve visual layout
while retaining selectable/copyable text where possible. Text is emitted as
HTML with generated CSS and web fonts. Non-text PDF content, fallback text, and
covered text are rendered into page background assets.

The project is mainly C++ with a C wrapper for FontForge internals. Python is
used for tests and helper scripts. Shell scripts drive the full build because
the converter is tightly coupled to specific Poppler and FontForge versions.

The project wiki has been read and localized into local docs. User-facing wiki
material lives mainly under `docs/user/`, package/container notes in
`docs/build/prebuilt-packages.md`, and page provenance in
`docs/reference/wiki-sources.md`. Treat explicitly old wiki pages as historical
context unless current repository files corroborate them.

## Current Architecture Summary

Runtime flow:

1. `pdf2htmlEX/src/pdf2htmlEX.cc` parses CLI options into `Param`.
2. Poppler `PDFDocFactory` opens the PDF.
3. `HTMLRenderer::process` runs `Preprocessor` as a first Poppler `OutputDev`
   pass to collect used font codes and max page dimensions.
4. `HTMLRenderer` runs as the main Poppler `OutputDev` pass.
5. Text callbacks populate `HTMLTextPage` and `HTMLTextLine`.
6. Font callbacks install, extract, reencode, hint, and emit fonts/CSS through
   `HTMLRenderer/font.cc` and `util/ffw.c`.
7. Drawing/image callbacks feed `DrawingTracer` and `CoveredTextDetector`.
8. `BackgroundRenderer` renders PNG/JPEG/SVG page backgrounds.
9. `StateManager` emits reusable CSS classes.
10. `share/manifest` assembles final HTML from static assets and generated
    CSS/pages/outline.

There is no full document IR. The code uses per-document maps, per-page text
buffers, CSS class managers, and temporary output streams.

## Main Directories And Files

- `README.md`: root overview and patched behavior notes.
- `docs/README.md`: documentation index.
- `docs/user/`: quick start, browser/mobile, optimization/customization, FAQ,
  and limitations localized from the wiki.
- `docs/reference/wiki-sources.md`: wiki page inventory and caveats.
- `buildScripts/`: active build/test/release shell scripts.
- `pdf2htmlEX/CMakeLists.txt`: converter CMake project and source list.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/pdf2htmlEX.cc`: CLI and program entry point.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/Param.h`: runtime configuration.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/ArgParser.*`: `getopt_long` wrapper.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/Preprocessor.*`: first PDF scan.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/HTMLRenderer.h`: central renderer.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/general.cc`: process loop and manifest assembly.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/state.cc`: PDF state to HTML state conversion.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/text.cc`: text extraction/output buffering.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/font.cc`: font extraction/conversion/CSS.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/image.cc`: image coverage tracing.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/draw.cc`: path/stroke/fill tracing.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/link.cc`: link annotation output.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/form.cc`: optional form output.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/outline.cc`: outline output.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/BackgroundRenderer/`: Splash and Cairo background renderers.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLTextPage.*`: per-page text collection and clipping.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLTextLine.*`: line-level HTML text and spans.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/StateManager.h`: CSS class interning.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/CoveredTextDetector.*`: covered character decisions.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/DrawingTracer.*`: Cairo geometry tracing.
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/util/ffw.*`: FontForge C wrapper.
- `pdf2htmlEX/share/manifest`: final HTML template.
- `pdf2htmlEX/share/*.in`: CSS/JS templates.
- `pdf2htmlEX/test/`: Python tests and fixtures.
- `poppler/`, `fontforge/`, `poppler-data/`: dependency source/data trees.

## Build Summary

Normal build path is script-driven:

```sh
./buildScripts/buildInstallLocallyApt
```

The wiki strongly recommends this scripted path because Poppler and FontForge
must be matched to `pdf2htmlEX` source expectations.

Equivalent sequence:

```sh
./buildScripts/versionEnvs
./buildScripts/reportEnvs
./buildScripts/getBuildToolsApt
./buildScripts/getDevLibrariesApt
./buildScripts/getPoppler
./buildScripts/buildPoppler
./buildScripts/getFontforge
./buildScripts/buildFontforge
./buildScripts/buildPdf2htmlEX
./buildScripts/installPdf2htmlEX
```

Current version pins in `buildScripts/versionEnvs`:

- `PDF2HTMLEX_VERSION=0.18.8.rc2`
- `POPPLER_VERSION=poppler-24.06.1`
- `FONTFORGE_VERSION=20230101`

`getPoppler` downloads `poppler-data-0.4.12`.

Local verification:

- `pdf2htmlEX/build/pdf2htmlEX -v` ran and reported Poppler `24.06.1`,
  FontForge `20230101`, Cairo `1.18.4`, and formats `png jpg svg`.
- `cmake --build pdf2htmlEX/build --target pdf2htmlEX -- -j2` failed before
  compilation with CMake `4.3.0`, because the project declares
  `cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 2.6.0)`.

Do not claim a clean build passes until the CMake policy issue is addressed or
the build is run in a compatible environment.

Prebuilt/wiki artifact docs:

- `docs/build/prebuilt-packages.md`
- Debian archive: install local `.deb` with `sudo apt install ./file.deb`.
- Docker wiki image name: `pdf2htmlex/pdf2htmlex`.
- Docker working directory in wiki examples: `/pdf`.
- Docker data dir in wiki examples: `/usr/local/share/pdf2htmlEX`.
- AppImage and `.deb` still rely on host Fontconfig/iconv configuration.

## Test Summary

Fast tests:

```sh
mkdir -p /tmp/pdf2htmlEX/tmp /tmp/pdf2htmlEX/dat /tmp/pdf2htmlEX/png /tmp/pdf2htmlEX/out /tmp/pdf2htmlEX/html
cd pdf2htmlEX/test
python3 test_output.py
```

Verified result: passed, 23 tests.

CTest:

```sh
ctest --test-dir pdf2htmlEX/build -N
ctest --test-dir pdf2htmlEX/build -R test_basic --output-on-failure
```

Verified result: CTest lists `test_basic` and `test_browser`; `test_basic`
passed.

Browser tests:

```sh
cd pdf2htmlEX/test
./runLocalBrowserTests
```

Not run during documentation because Selenium, geckodriver, and Xvfb were
missing locally. `browser_tests/text_visibility` is skipped in code due clipping
issues.

## Rendering Pipeline Summary

Input:

- CLI options
- input PDF
- data directory assets
- poppler-data

Processing:

- Parse CLI into `Param`.
- Open `PDFDoc`.
- Preprocess selected pages at `DEFAULT_DPI` to record used font codes and max
  page dimensions.
- Compute zoom/text scale factors.
- For each page, render through `HTMLRenderer` callbacks.
- Store text in `HTMLTextPage`/`HTMLTextLine`.
- Trace coverage with `DrawingTracer`/`CoveredTextDetector`.
- Render page background through Splash or Cairo.
- Emit generated CSS through `StateManager` and font code.
- Emit outlines, links, forms, and page metadata.
- Apply `share/manifest`.

Output:

- main HTML
- optional CSS file
- optional font files
- optional background files
- optional outline file
- optional split page files

Common wiki recipes:

- `pdf2htmlEX --zoom 1.3 input.pdf`
- `pdf2htmlEX -f 3 -l 5 --fit-width 1024 --bg-format jpg input.pdf`
- `pdf2htmlEX --embed cfijo --dest-dir out input.pdf`
- `pdf2htmlEX --embed cfijo --split-pages 1 --page-filename page-%d.page input.pdf`
- `pdf2htmlEX --fallback 1 input.pdf`

## Important Classes And Modules

`Param`
: All runtime options. Add fields here when adding CLI-configurable behavior.

`ArgParser`
: Typed CLI registration. Options are registered in `parse_options`.

`Preprocessor`
: First-pass scanner for font code maps and max dimensions.

`HTMLRenderer`
: Main coordinator and Poppler `OutputDev`. Changes here can affect everything.

`BackgroundRenderer`
: Strategy base class for background output.

`SplashBackgroundRenderer`
: PNG/JPEG renderer.

`CairoBackgroundRenderer`
: SVG renderer and external JPEG extraction path.

`HTMLTextPage`
: Page-level text buffer and clip grouping.

`HTMLTextLine`
: Line-level Unicode, offsets, state spans, and text optimization.

`StateManager`
: Interns CSS values into compact classes.

`CoveredTextDetector`
: Maintains per-character coverage flags.

`DrawingTracer`
: Tracks geometry for characters and later non-text drawing.

`ffw_*`
: FontForge wrapper functions for load, reencode, metric, hint, save.

## C++ And Python Interaction

The converter runtime is C++/C. Python tests invoke the built binary. Python
does not import converter modules or participate in conversion.

`test.py.in` configures the binary path and temp dirs. `test_output.py` runs the
binary and checks generated files. Browser tests use Selenium/Pillow screenshots.

## Known Risky Areas

- CMake/build-system compatibility with modern CMake.
- Poppler API/ABI changes.
- FontForge internal API changes.
- `HTMLRenderer/font.cc`, especially CID/TrueType/ToUnicode handling.
- `util/ffw.c`, because it uses FontForge internals and process-global state.
- Text positioning in `state.cc` and `HTMLTextLine.cc`.
- Covered-text detection and DPI changes.
- SVG output and fallback behavior.
- Manifest/data-dir assumptions.
- Browser tests, because they depend on external browser tooling.
- Generated files from CMake/resource minification.
- Custom manifests/data directories, because the wiki calls manifest syntax
  experimental.
- Mobile output performance, because the wiki says mobile devices are not
  officially supported.
- Reflowable text requests, because the converter targets fixed-layout fidelity.

## Recommended Workflow For Future AI Agents

1. Read `README.md`.
2. Read `docs/README.md`.
3. If the change is user-facing, read `docs/user/` and
   `docs/reference/wiki-sources.md`.
4. Inspect the relevant internals doc for the target behavior.
5. Inspect nearby source files and tests.
6. Make minimal changes.
7. Run targeted tests.
8. Update docs if behavior changes.

Always run `git status --short` before and after changes. Do not revert user
changes. Do not modify source code for documentation-only tasks unless required
to keep documentation links or generated docs valid.
